_doc: M19 3-case fail-fast verdict manifest. Validation = 3 cases (NOT 30); the principal runs more after v0.1.0. END-OF-CORE-DEV stamp, not a gate that slows core dev. All CPU-WRF truth is REUSED from the existing corpus -- NO new WRF runs. Verification domain is d02 (3 km nest, 159x66x44) for every case/level; the GPU operational scorer (proofs/coupled/task2_skill_signal.py) reads wrfout_d02 at the valid time. The GPU init source is the t=0 wrfout_d02 snapshot of each run dir (build_replay_case uses run.history_files(domain)[0]; boundary leaves need >=2 history files -- satisfied: every chosen run has >=25 hourly d02 files).
_corpus_finding: Most run dirs under wrf_l2/wrf_l3 contain ONLY static-table + met_em symlinks and ZERO wrfout output (purged or never-completed overnight runs). Only a handful of runs retain real hourly wrfout history. The chosen 3 cases are ALL of the corpus inits that retain a complete, contiguous d02 history at the leads M19 needs.
_fail_fast_spec: Run Case 1 FIRST on BOTH L2 and L3. If it FAILS on either -> STOP (do not run cases 2/3) and emit a probe-pointer (which field/lead/level failed). If Case 1 PASSES on both, run cases 2 and 3 to CONFIRM. 24-72h is the real gate (+1h/+3h already validated, see proofs/coupled/task2_skill_signal_{1,3}h.json).
_l2_vs_l3: L2 and L3 share an IDENTICAL d02 nest geometry (159x66, DX=3000 m, 44 levels) and the same GPU forecast config; they differ in the parent configuration / max lead (L2 = 72h forecast, L3 = 24h triple/penta-nest forecast). Case 1 must pass on BOTH the L2 d02 and the L3 d02 corpus solutions for the same init -- i.e. the GPU forecast initialized from each family's own t=0 d02 snapshot, scored against that family's d02 history.

verification.truth_source: corpus CPU-WRF wrfout_d02 (Gen2 NVHPC build), REUSED

cases.synoptic_note: Strongest-flow regime of the three: 10 m wind mean 7.4 m/s (max 12.8), NE-to-SW flow (U10mean -2.9, V10mean -6.5), T2 mean 293.4 K range [291.5,296.6]. Chosen as Case 1 because it is the freshest full-coverage corpus init AND the most dynamically stressful -> best early-failure detector.

Distinct weak-flow regime: 10 m wind mean 4.5 m/s (max 8.1), SE flow with REVERSED zonal sign vs Case 1 (U10mean +3.0, V10mean -3.1), cooler/wider T2 [285.1,294.1]. 19 days before Case 1 -> independent synoptic situation. Has full L2 (72h) AND full L3 (24h), so it can also exercise the long-lead path if the manager wants a second 72h confirmation.

Moderate-flow regime: 10 m wind mean 5.8 m/s (max 10.0), U10mean +1.0, V10mean -5.4, T2 [289.8,297.1]. This is the EXACT pin used for the already-validated +1h/+3h GPU preview (proofs/coupled/task2_skill_signal_{1,3}h.json: T2 RMSE 1.33/1.83, U10 2.22/1.90, V10 3.69/2.75), so it gives direct continuity / regression-anchor with the prior preview. L3-only at full coverage (its L2 sibling run is a partial 20h history), so case3 confirmation is L3 24h only.

rejected_or_unavailable.note: Inits considered but lacking full d02 coverage at M19 leads.
rejected_or_unavailable.20260521_18z_l2_72h: partial: only 20 hourly d02 (+0..+19h); cannot serve the 24-72h L2 gate -> case3 is L3-only.
rejected_or_unavailable.20260528_18z_l3 sibling timestamps: all other 20260528 L3/L2 run-timestamps either 0 outputs or superseded by the 20260529T002423Z full run.
rejected_or_unavailable.all_zero_output_dirs: ~50 corpus run dirs contain only static-table+met_em symlinks and ZERO wrfout (purged/incomplete); unusable.
